_description: WSET-AI-System Retrieval Priority Matrix. Defines retrieval weighting, agent eligibility, and calibration safety for each source type in the system. This matrix is the operational implementation of the trust tier architecture defined in SEMANTIC_RETRIEVAL_ARCHITECTURE.md. Each entry represents one source type. Retrieval systems must consult this matrix when composing agent context windows.

source_types.label: WSET Official Mark Scheme
source_types.notes: Tutor Agent may reference for factual correctness anchoring but must not reproduce mark scheme language verbatim to learners. Examiner Agent's primary scoring source. Requires calibration gate sign-off. Session-specific mark schemes have expiry dates.

source_types.label: WSET Examiner Guidance Notes
source_types.notes: Chief examiner reports and marking guidance. Examiner Agent uses for mark scheme interpretation. Tutor Agent may use to understand what examiners expect, but must not represent this as learner-facing guidance without abstraction.

source_types.label: WSET Grade Descriptors (P/M/D)
source_types.notes: Official Pass/Merit/Distinction criteria. Both agents may use. Tutor Agent uses for distinction_training role; Examiner Agent uses for grade band determination. Not session-specific; valid for the curriculum period.

source_types.label: WSET Level 3 Award in Wines Textbook
source_types.notes: Primary Tutor Agent knowledge source. All knowledge graph concept nodes are anchored to this source. Examiner Agent uses only for contextual interpretation of mark scheme terms — not as primary scoring authority. Must be current edition.

source_types.label: WSET Official SAT Documentation
source_types.notes: The SAT framework has standalone scoring authority for tasting-based assessments. Examiner weight equals Tier 0 for tasting questions specifically. Tutor Agent must use SAT vocabulary exclusively for tasting_alignment pedagogical role.

source_types.label: Validated Benchmark Answers (L4-reviewed)
source_types.notes: Examiner weight capped at 0.70. Examiner Agent may use as calibration reference but never as primary scoring authority. Must carry individual L4 human validation sign-off. Not the same as question bank entries without review.

source_types.label: Internal Question Bank (wset3_question_bank.jsonl)
source_types.notes: The internal question bank (source_trust_tier: internal_training) is NOT examiner_eligible. It contains questions constructed without WSET mark scheme alignment confirmation. Tutor Agent may use for practice question generation and weakness-targeted retrieval. calibration_safe: false because bank contains items with needs_human_review=true flags.

source_types.label: Wine With Jimmy Transcripts
source_types.notes: Informal pedagogical enrichment. Examiner weight is 0.00 — absolutely excluded from any scoring context. Tutor Agent access requires: (a) human review pass with WSET accuracy check, (b) enrichment_flag=true annotation, (c) mandatory framing statement to learner. Must not be used for exam strategy guidance.

source_types.label: WSET Diploma (L4) Content as L3 Enrichment
source_types.notes: Diploma content may only be used in the Tutor Agent after explicit L3-downgrade annotation confirming the content is coherent with L3 scope. Must not introduce Diploma-only marking criteria or Diploma-level expectations. Requires framing statement. FORBIDDEN for Examiner Agent regardless of annotation.

source_types.label: Knowledge Graph — Concept Nodes
source_types.notes: Concept nodes are pedagogically authored and are not aligned to WSET marking authority. High Tutor Agent priority. The +0.10 graph traversal bonus applies when retrieved via graph (not dense search). Retrieval priority elevated to 3 despite Tier 4 because graph structure provides structural value beyond raw trust tier.

source_types.label: Knowledge Graph — Misconception Nodes
source_types.notes: Misconception nodes are forced-retrieved (not scored) when a detection_signal match occurs. Tutor weight 0.85 reflects high pedagogical value. Retrieval priority 2 because misconception correction always takes priority over content delivery. Examiner Agent never engages in misconception correction.

source_types.label: Knowledge Graph — Causal Chain Nodes
source_types.notes: Causal chains encode Distinction-level mechanistic knowledge. High Tutor Agent value for distinction_training and reinforcement roles. The +0.10 graph traversal bonus applies. Examiner Agent forbidden — pedagogical depth content is not marking authority.

source_types.label: AI-Generated Content (Tier 5)
source_types.notes: Lowest trust tier. Must be labelled as AI-generated and must not be presented to learners as authoritative. graph_eligible: false — generated content is not admitted to the knowledge graph until human-reviewed and promoted. Tutor Agent may use only when no higher-tier source is available and with appropriate framing.
